description: Locally defined `escapeHtml` follows the common naming convention; it matches the JS Sanitizer(Cap::HTML_ESCAPE) label rule via suffix word-boundary. Calling it before an innerHTML sink should strip HTML_ESCAPE so the downstream concatenation is no longer an XSS.

expected.notes: escapeHtml is matched as Sanitizer(Cap::HTML_ESCAPE); innerHTML of the escaped value should not fire taint-unsanitised-flow
